source: RMC-BestFit/src/RMC.BestFit/Estimation/BayesianAnalysis.cs @ fc28c0c (ComputeLeverageDiagnostics 1955 / ComputeInfluenceDiagnostics 1870 / ComputePriorInfluenceDiagnostics 1927). D6 EMITTER-VERIFIED: values reproduced against the real RMC.BestFit.Diagnostics classes (LeverageDiagnostics / InfluenceDiagnostics / PriorInfluenceDiagnostics compiled in-place by the oracle emitter). Drives a seeded Bayesian DEMCzs MCMC over a Normal UnivariateDistributionModel, then computes all three diagnostics off that single fit. Tolerance tiers: structural counts + obs_value == equal; posterior-deterministic PSIS/prior quantities (mean_pareto_k, pareto_k, elpd_loo, total_data_log_likelihood, mean_prior_precision_share, proportion_problematic, max_pareto_k) pinned at rel/abs 1e-9 (the seeded posterior is bit-identical C#-vs-C++, reproduced to ~1e-12); MAP-point leverage quantities (total_leverage/fit/variance + obs_*) stay at rel 1e-3 -- the C#-vs-C++ finite-difference Hessian + regularized-inverse noise floor is ~7.6e-4 (well above the brief's optimistic 1e-5..1e-8 Hessian tier), so 1e-3 is the tightest that reproduces cross-implementation. thinning_interval pinned to 1; short run (iterations 100, output_length 400). prior_influence_count / total_prior_log_likelihood / prior_to_data_ratio now reproduce the C# oracle (3 distinctly-named prior components -- Parameter Prior: Mean, Parameter Prior: Std Dev, Jeffreys Scale): X1 ported Distribution.ParameterNames onto the univariate distribution base and wired it through the trend/ModelParameter owner-name path, so the two Normal parameter-prior components no longer collapse under the name-keyed dedup in PriorInfluenceDiagnostics (which faithfully mirrors the C# Dictionary-by-name collection). Values reproduced against the real RMC.BestFit.PriorInfluenceDiagnostics by the dotnet oracle gate.
